claims.substrate: SU(2) Wilson lattice gauge theory on Z^4 at fixed lattice spacing a > 0
claims.projection: transfer gap above vacuum in infinite-volume gauge-invariant Osterwalder-Seiler reflection-positive Hilbert space
claims.statement: The theorem-track anchor is a fixed-spacing strong-coupling SU(2) transfer-gap claim for beta < beta* = 0.006296889394074993, not a continuum Yang-Mills construction.
boundary: Wilson lattice gauge theory on Z^4 only.
No continuum construction, weak-coupling control, asymptotic-freedom trajectory, SU(N>=3) extension, or Clay result is claimed.

claims.projection: typed bridge failures between fixed-spacing strong coupling and Clay continuum target
claims.statement: The obstruction-survey lane classifies why fixed-spacing strong-coupling evidence does not currently bridge to the Clay continuum problem.
boundary: Taxonomy only; not a bridge proof.
Every missing bridge must remain assigned to an explicit obstruction wall.
No obstruction classification is allowed to count as a continuum construction.

claims.projection: Frobenius closure, residual defect control, and KP insertion candidate
claims.statement: Lane VII frames the current wall-moving candidate as arbitrary cyclic Frobenius closure plus residual defect control plus KP insertion, with candidate beta_Frob ~= 0.00735851805 remaining conditional.
boundary: No arbitrary cyclic Frobenius closure theorem is claimed.
No KP insertion theorem is claimed.
No residual defect subcriticality theorem is claimed.
No beta-wall movement is claimed as a theorem.
No continuum or Clay claim is made.

claims.substrate: Yang-Mills execution workplan across v0.18.x, v0.14.4, and v0.9.20
claims.projection: Move 4 cross-reference pass, Move 1 arXiv/CMP submission package, Move 2 Wigner-symbol Jmax=1/2 derivation
claims.statement: YM-WP-v0.1 sequences Moves 4, 1, and 2 as execution planning only; it adds no new theorem and inherits all non-claim boxes from v0.14.4, v0.18.1, and v0.9.20.
boundary: Workplan only; no new mathematical result is asserted.
Move 4 is editorial cross-reference hardening from v0.18.1 to v0.14.4.
Move 1 is submission packaging for v0.14.4 and companion survey after cross-reference hardening.
Move 2 is symbolic Wigner derivation / structural-zero gate work for v0.9.20 -> v0.9.21.
No continuum construction, weak-coupling theorem, SU(N>=3) extension, or Clay claim is made.

gates.expected: The v0.14.4 theorem-track claim remains fixed-spacing, SU(2), beta<beta*, and non-continuum.
KP certificate status is recorded without implying weak-coupling or continuum control.
Fixed-spacing evidence is not promoted to continuum construction.
Every missing bridge is typed by a declared obstruction wall.
No bridge is left as untyped prose.
Lane VII Frobenius closure remains conditional until proved for arbitrary cyclic channel graphs.
Residual defect control remains explicit and typed.
KP insertion of any hybrid activity remains conditional until proved.
YM-WP-v0.1 remains planning-only and is not theorem-track promotion.
Open PRs #22/#23 are tracked as backlog inputs and not treated as merged theorem inputs.
G1/G2 Wigner-symbol gates remain open until v0.9.21 supplies symbolic 5x5 derivation and Phi_111 selection-rule result.
Validates the next-tranche scaffold and emits digest-addressed events without proving closure, moving a wall, or claiming continuum Yang-Mills.

non_claims.statement: This adapter does not claim a solution of the Clay Yang-Mills existence and mass gap problem.
This adapter does not promote fixed-spacing, finite-cutoff, finite-channel, or planning evidence to a continuum construction.
Every missing bridge must remain assigned to an explicit obstruction wall.
This adapter does not claim weak-coupling or asymptotic-freedom control.
This adapter does not generalize the SU(2) fixed-spacing result to SU(N>=3).
This adapter does not claim arbitrary cyclic Frobenius closure.
The candidate beta_Frob wall is conditional and not a theorem until Frobenius closure, residual defect control, and KP insertion pass controller-witnessed gates.
YM-WP-v0.1 is an execution plan only and asserts no new mathematical result.
This adapter does not mark G1/G2 Wigner-symbol gates closed until v0.9.21 provides the symbolic 5x5 matrix and Phi_111 selection-rule result.
